skill.name: convex
skill.description: Routes general Convex requests to the right project skill. Use when the user asks which Convex skill to use or gives an underspecified Convex app task.
skill.summary: Routes Convex requests to specific project skills for authentication, migrations, performance audits, and more.
skill.author: get-convex

security_audit.summary: Evaluated all static findings. The reported patterns are false positives: markdown code fences were misidentified as shell backtick execution, documentation URLs were flagged as hardcoded URLs, and YAML frontmatter was misidentified as cryptographic code. This is a routing skill that provides guidance documentation with no actual security risks.

medium_findings.title: Static Scanner Misidentification
medium_findings.description: External command detection on lines 21, 23, 35, 41-44 is a false positive. The scanner detected markdown code fence syntax (backticks) and npm command references as shell execution patterns. These are legitimate documentation elements: `npx convex ai-files install` is standard npm tooling, and skill names like `convex-quickstart` are documentation references.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown code fences use backticks for syntax highlighting, npm commands are legitimate tooling references, skill names are documentation text. No actual shell execution exists in this file.

medium_findings.title: Static Scanner Misidentification
medium_findings.description: Network URL detection on lines 29, 33 is a false positive. The flagged URLs `https://docs.convex.dev/ai` and `https://convex.link/convex_rules.txt` are legitimate Convex documentation URLs, not malicious endpoints.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are official Convex documentation URLs pointing to public developer resources, not exfiltration endpoints.

medium_findings.title: Static Scanner Misidentification
medium_findings.description: Cryptographic algorithm detection on lines 3, 27 is a false positive. The scanner misidentified YAML frontmatter delimiters and documentation text as cryptographic patterns. This file contains only markdown documentation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: YAML frontmatter uses --- delimiters which are not cryptographic. No cryptographic algorithms or implementations exist in this documentation file.

content.user_title: Route Convex tasks to the right project skill
content.value_statement: Developers working with Convex need help identifying which specific skill handles their task. This routing skill directs users to specialized skills for authentication setup, migrations, performance audits, and more.
actual_capabilities: Routes requests to specialized Convex skills based on user intent
Installs Convex AI guidance files using npx convex ai-files install
Directs users to convex-quickstart for new projects
Routes authentication setup tasks to convex-setup-auth
limitations: Only routes to other Convex skills, does not execute Convex code directly
Does not provide Convex API documentation itself
Cannot install Convex projects without Node.js and npm

use_cases.title: Starting a new Convex project
use_cases.description: A developer wants to add Convex backend to their React app. This routing skill directs them to the convex-quickstart skill for step-by-step setup guidance.

use_cases.title: Adding authentication to existing Convex app
use_cases.description: A developer needs to add user authentication. This routing skill redirects them to convex-setup-auth for authentication implementation guidance.
use_cases.target_user: Developers adding auth to existing projects

use_cases.title: Planning a database migration
use_cases.description: A developer needs to migrate Convex schema or data. This routing skill directs them to convex-migration-helper for migration planning and execution.

prompt_templates.title: Start new project with Convex
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to add Convex to my React application. What should I do first?
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to start using Convex in a new or existing project

prompt_templates.title: Add user authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I add authentication to my Convex backend?
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs to implement user login or signup

prompt_templates.title: Build reusable component
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to create a reusable Convex component for my codebase.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to build modular Convex code

prompt_templates.title: Fix performance issues
prompt_templates.prompt: My Convex queries are running slowly. How can I investigate performance?
prompt_templates.scenario: When user encounters slow database operations

output_examples.input: I want to add Convex to my app
output_examples.output:
To get started with Convex, I recommend installing the Convex AI guidance first:

```bash
npx convex ai-files install
```

Then use the convex-quickstart skill for detailed setup instructions for new projects.

output_examples.input: How do I set up authentication?
output_examples.output: For authentication setup in Convex, I recommend switching to the convex-setup-auth skill which provides step-by-step guidance for implementing user authentication in your Convex backend.

best_practices: Run npx convex ai-files install first to get official Convex AI guidance in your project
Use the most specific Convex skill for your task rather than staying in the general routing skill
Check if another Convex skill already matches your request before asking for routing
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill when you already know which Convex workflow you need
Do not manually copy Convex rules when npx convex ai-files install is available
Do not stay in this routing skill when a more specific skill matches your request

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: This is a routing skill that directs you to the right specialized Convex skill based on your task.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: Run npx convex ai-files install to get official Convex AI guidance files in your project.

faq.question: What Convex skills are available?
faq.answer: Skills include convex-quickstart, convex-setup-auth, convex-create-component, convex-migration-helper, and convex-performance-audit.

faq.question: Can I use this without Convex installed?
faq.answer: Yes, but you will need Node.js and npm to run npx convex ai-files install.

faq.question: When should I switch to another skill?
faq.answer: Switch when you have a specific task like authentication, migrations, or performance that matches another Convex skill.

faq.question: Is this skill official Convex tooling?
faq.answer: This skill is maintained by get-convex and provides guidance for using Convex with AI coding tools.
